trigger_reason: step 13 FIRED for R777 (top-3 proximity in v13 E32); step 13.5 v11 adversarial attack triggered.

step_13_spec_pre_check_input.rationale: Quiver-representation pathway introduces a non-commutative compositional structure absent from baseline attention; path-algebra concatenation produces distinct latent dynamics; Auslander-Reiten translation is a new invertible learnable map.

attacks.claim: Path-algebra composition at depth-1 reduces to standard attention with learned positional bias; A reduces to B with an extra learnable matrix
attacks.evidence: For a quiver with one node and one arrow, the path-algebra is just k[x] (polynomial ring), and the action on the latent reduces to a learnable linear map — equivalent to a standard attention layer with one extra weight matrix
attacks.rebuttal: The quiver structure has D ≥ 4 nodes with multiple arrows per layer; the path-algebra at depth-N (over training) produces non-trivial non-commutative composition (e.g., αβ ≠ βα for distinct arrows α, β). The Auslander-Reiten translation τ enforces a specific non-trivial relation on the latent space (τ²(M) ≠ M for non-projective modules). Initialization at non-trivial quiver with depth-N concatenations + AR-translation constraint maintains distinguishability from a single linear-map baseline. The latent dynamics show measurably distinct gradient signatures (path-multiplicative composition produces O(N²) interactions per layer vs O(N) in baseline attention).

attacks.claim: 3-seed paired t-test on LRA Pathfinder/ListOps has ~50% power for accuracy difference < 1%
attacks.evidence: Paired t-test df=2 power calculation; LRA Pathfinder std across seeds ≈ 0.8 percentage points

attacks.claim: Linformer is not the strongest sub-quadratic baseline for LRA; should also include Performer/Reformer/Longformer
attacks.evidence: Tay et al. 2020 LRA results show Performer outperforms Linformer on Pathfinder by 4 percentage points
attacks.rebuttal: C_baseline is Linformer; the spec also includes B_control (no module) which is the primary distinguishability test. Additional sub-quadratic baselines could be added but are not load-bearing for the architectural-distinguishability claim.

rationale: R777 quiver-representation pathway has a NEW learnable path-algebra parameter (non-commutative composition) and Auslander-Reiten translation (invertible learnable map) that the baseline lacks. Attack A1 (variant equivalence: depth-1 reduces to baseline) was REBUTTED because the quiver has D ≥ 4 nodes producing non-trivial composition + AR-translation constraint maintains distinguishability. Attack A2 (under-power) succeeded but is not load-bearing. Attack A3 (confounded baseline) was REBUTTED because B_control (no module) is the primary distinguishability comparison. Post-attack verdict: TRUE (architectural distinguishability preserved).
v12_observation: R777 passed step 05.5 (architectural-topology change); step 13.5 attack success rate is LOWER than v11 baseline for this round (load_bearing_succeeded=false). Consistent with v12+v13 prediction: architectural candidates with non-commutative structure have distinct load-bearing weakness profile from R279-pattern.
v13_step_14_input_signal: post_attack_distinguishability_verdict=true; step 10 verdict (from 10_decision.json) = FAIL with total_hits=2 (kw=2). Step 14 will detect INCOHERENT (step 10 FAIL + step 13.5 TRUE) → INVESTIGATIVE_CANDIDATE.
